text: 英語 聽力 模擬 視題
十三
第一節
聽下面五段對話
每段對話後有一個小題
從題中所給的
A B C 3個選項中選出最佳選項
聽完每段對話後
你都有十秒鐘的時間
來回答有關小題和閱讀像小題
每段對話僅讀一遍
現在你有五秒鐘的時間
閱讀第一小題的有關內容
Oh Jack, you're wet through to the skin
The weather man said it would be sunny today
so I went out for a walk without taking an umbrella
the weather is so changeable
Where is Chris? He isn't in the office
I guess he is buying some snacks and decorations
There's a birthday party for some employees this afternoon
How has Sarah been doing since she started her new job?
She's doing well
At first, she was quite nervous
but her confidence grew with the passage of time
That's great to hear
She doesn't disappoint me
What optional course are you taking this term?
History of art
I took history of music and environmental science last term
Oh really? We'll be in the same class then
I felt like a fool when I failed the math exam again
Don't blame yourself
You studied as hard as you could
Maybe you just need to change your study approaches
The first step is to the end
The second step
Listen to the 5th step of math and math
Each of the math and math and math have a few small questions
From the answer given by the answer
A, B, C, and three questions
choose the most questions
Listen to the math and math
You'll have time to read each of the small questions
Each small question is five seconds
This does look like a nice place to catch up with friends, like you said, John
Do you often eat here?
No, I've only been here a few times with my colleague
Well, what are you going to drink?
I'm going to have a glass of soda water
All right, the steak here is great
And so is the spaghetti
I highly recommend you try them
I'm not that hungry
So I'll have the steak and a salad
Okay, I'll get a waiter to help us

Luke, do you want to get a pet dog?
I know some dogs need to be adopted
Of course, I've always dreamed of having one
My aunt has an alcation and a mastiff
But she is going to settle in Australia
And she's too old to take care of them
Will you keep one of them?
Both are good watchdogs
I'll have the alcation
Can you take me to your aunt's house tonight?
Sorry, I need to attend a music last night
How about tomorrow?
Deal

Frank, I heard you went to Harbing
Did you go there to see the ice sculptures?
I did see some fantastic ice sculptures
But I went there to volunteer for the 2025 Asian Winter Games
Asian Winter Games?
I've never heard of it before
Well, the Asian Winter Games
is an international, multi-sport event
held every four years for members of the Olympic Council of Asia
featuring winter events
The 2025 Asian Winter Games was held in Harbing
It started on February 7th and ended on February 14th
And for the first time, Cambodia and Saudi Arabia sent athletes to the Asian Winter Games
Well, I'm from Denmark
So I'm not familiar with Asian Games
How many countries participated in the Games?
Around 30 countries in regions I'd say
Wow, it must have been a big event
When did you arrive in Harbing?
I arrived a day before the Asian Winter Games
Then what kind of volunteer work did you do?
There were lots of athletes and officials from different countries
My job was to help them talk to the people organizing the event
What was the most unforgettable moment for you?
I watched a speed skating event
and got a signed photograph from my favorite skater
It sounds like you had a wonderful time

Hey Estelle, I've been thinking about improving my writing lately
Do you have any tips?
Absolutely
Before taking steps to improve it
You should know what writing skills specifically include
Grammar, vocabulary, spelling and structure, I think
Yes, but there's more to it than just those
There's accuracy and clarity
Each of these elements can influence the quality of writing
I see, so I need to take those elements into account when I write
That's right, but you should find out the key elements first
Grammar and spelling form the foundation of good writing
After finishing your text, you can review what you've written to correct errors
And asking for feedback is a great way to see how others will interpret your text
Those are good tips
Professor Smith once said that my paper was submitted too quickly
and needed many changes
Well, what are some good ways to perfect the text?
You can avoid using the passive voice
Adjust sentence length and choose strong verbs
I see, thanks for the advice
You've been really helpful

The phrase getting out of your comfort zone
is thrown around so much today that it is losing its meaning
This phrase seems to cover up what we don't want to talk about
Fear
Let me tell you how fear has played a role in my journey
I come from a family of travelers
My mom started a travel agency when she was younger than I am now
and my parents never left to me behind when they went on their adventures
I graduated early and got a degree from college by the time I turned 18
And at that time, I had traveled to around 70 countries
This was the point in my life where people began to ask me the scariest question
What are you going to do next?
To answer that question, I began asking myself what I was most interested in
which has always been traveling
and how to make the most out of the cards that I was dealt
which was how much travel experience I'd had at my age
That's when I realized I had over six years to break the world record
for the youngest person to travel to every country
